description: In Python, never hand-edit outputs from protoc, pydantic codegen, or setuptools entry-point generation; change the source and rerun the generator.
body: In Python, outputs from protoc, pydantic codegen, or setuptools entry-point generation are derived artifacts: editing them directly drifts from the generator and is overwritten on the next run. Use when a diff touches generated files, codegen inputs, or the the interpreter version, the active venv, and the dependency resolver that produces them. Do not use this to justify patching generated output by hand or skipping regeneration. Actions: locate the generator and its inputs, edit the source, rerun the generation step, then run the package's editable install (pip install -e / uv sync) inside its venv so the regenerated output is what ships. Validation signals include a clean regeneration diff, a passing pytest on the changed package inside the project venv, and no manual edits left in the generated tree. Risk boundary: escalate before regenerating if the generator is pinned, vendored, or owned by another team, and roll back if regeneration changes unrelated files.
